title: Peppery Gooey German Vegetables Stir-fry
description: An energizing German dish featuring sauerkraut and other vegetables cooked in a peppery sauce with a gooey texture for a vegetarian-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: nutritional yeast
ingredients.amount: optional, to enhance the flavor and texture

instructions: Peel and dice carrots, onions, and peppers into similar sizes.
In a large pan or wok, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add ginger slices and garlic cloves; cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add chopped sauerkraut to the pan and stir for 2-3 minutes until slightly softened but still crunchy.
Add diced carrots, onions, peppers, and a pinch of red pepper flakes into the pan. Cook for another 5-7 minutes until vegetables start to soften.
In a small bowl, mix soy sauce with a dash of salt and black pepper. Pour this mixture over the vegetables in the pan.
Stir well to combine all ingredients and ensure they're evenly coated with the sauce. Let it cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until everything is heated through and slightly gooey.
If desired, sprinkle some nutritional yeast on top before serving for a gooey texture.
